questions.explanation: As order of 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 matrix contains 9 elements. Each element can be selected in 2 ways (it can be either 0 or 1 ). Hence, all the nine netries can be chosen in 2 9 = 512 ways (by the multiplication principle)

questions.question_html: If the matrix A is both symmetric and skew symmetric, then
questions.explanation: Only a null matrix can be symmetric as well as skew symmetric. In Symmetric Matrix A T = A, Skew Symmetric Matrix A T = -A, Given that the matrix is satisfying both the properties. Therefore, Equating the RHS we get A = -A i.e, 2A = 0. Therefore A = 0, which is a null matrix.

questions.correct_option_text: AB may or may not be defined.
questions.explanation: Let matrix A is of order m x n and matrix B is of order p x q. Then, the product AB is defined only when number of columns in A is equal to number of rows in B i.e n = p. Therefore, If A and B are any two matrices, then AB may or may not be defined.

questions.explanation: We know that if A is non - singular matrix, then A(adjA) = |A| n-2 A, where n is order of square matrix A. questions.explanation: {tex}I = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore I\cos \theta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \theta }&0 \\ 0&{\cos \theta } \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}J = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1 \\ { - 1}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore j\sin \theta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{\sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore I\cos \theta + J\sin \theta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \theta }&1 \\ 0&{\cos \theta } \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{\sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \theta }&{\sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \end{array}} \right] = B{/tex}

questions.question_text: If A + B = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and A – 2 B = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , then A = ?
questions.question_html: If A + B = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and A – 2 B ={tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, then A = ?
questions.explanation: A + B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} ...(i) A - 2B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} ...(ii) adding 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (i) and (ii), we get 2A + 2B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\ 2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} ...(iii) A - 2B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} ...(iv) adding (iii) and (iv), we get {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & 0 \\ 2 & 2 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{1}{3}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

questions.explanation: {tex}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]_{3\times2}}\left\{ {{{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0 \\ 0 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]}_{2\times3}} - \;\;{{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0 \\ 1 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {23} \\ {21} \end{array}} \right]}_{2\times3}}} \right\}{/tex} {tex} = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]_{3\times2}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 1 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 0 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 21} \\ { - 20} \end{array}} \right]_{2\times3}}{/tex} {tex} = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ 2&0&{ - 40} \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 102} \end{array}} \right]_{3\times3}}{/tex}

questions.question_text: Let a, b, c, d, u, v be integers. If the system of equations, ax + by = u, cx + dy = v, has a unique solution in integers, then
questions.question_html: Let a, b, c, d, u, v be integers. If the system of equations, ax + by = u, cx + dy = v, has a unique solution in integers, then

options.text: ad - bc need not be equal to {tex} \pm 1{/tex} .
options.html: ad - bc need not be equal to {tex} \pm 1{/tex}.

questions.correct_option_text: ad - bc need not be equal to {tex} \pm 1{/tex} .
questions.explanation: ax + by = u , cx +dy = v, {tex}\Delta = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&b \\ c&d \end{array}} \right| = ad - bc;{/tex} {tex}{\Delta _1} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u&b \\ v&d \end{array}} \right| = ud - bv;{/tex} {tex}{\Delta _2} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&u \\ c&v \end{array}} \right| = av - cu;{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = \frac{{{\Delta _1}}}{\Delta } = \frac{{ud - bv}}{{ad - bc}},y = \frac{{{\Delta _2}}}{\Delta } = \frac{{av - cu}}{{ad - bc}}{/tex} since the solution is unique in integers. {tex}\begin{gathered} \Delta = \pm 1 \;, ad - bc = \pm 1 \\ \end{gathered} {/tex}

questions.question_text: For what value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} the following system of equations does not have a solution x + y + z = 6, 4x + {tex}\lambda {/tex} y - {tex}\lambda {/tex} z = 0, 3x + 2y – 4z = - 5 ?
questions.question_html: For what value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} the following system of equations does not have a solution x + y + z = 6, 4x + {tex}\lambda {/tex}y - {tex}\lambda {/tex}z = 0, 3x + 2y – 4z = - 5 ?
questions.explanation: The given system of equations does not have solution if {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 4&\lambda &{ - \lambda } \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \end{array}} \right| = 0 \Rightarrow \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&1 \\ {4 + \lambda }&{2\lambda }&{ - \lambda } \\ 7&6&4 \end{array}} \right| = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (24 + 6\lambda - 14\lambda ) = 0 \Rightarrow \lambda = 3 {/tex}

questions.question_text: The value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} , for which system of equations. x + y + z = 1, x + 2y + 2z = 3, x + 2y + {tex}\lambda {/tex} z = 4, have no solution is
questions.question_html: The value of {tex}\lambda {/tex}, for which system of equations. x + y + z = 1, x + 2y + 2z = 3, x + 2y + {tex}\lambda {/tex}z = 4, have no solution is
questions.explanation: The given system of equations does not have solution if : {tex}\left| {\matrix{ 1 & 1 & 1 \cr 1 & 2 & 2 \cr 1 & 2 & \lambda \cr } } \right| \buildrel\textstyle.\over= 0 \Rightarrow 1(2\lambda - 4) - 1(\lambda - 2) + 1(2 - 2) = 0 \Rightarrow \lambda = 2{/tex}

questions.question_text: If the system of equations x + 4ay + az = 0, x + 3by + bz = 0 and x + 2 cy +cz = 0 have a non-zero solution,then a, b, c are in
questions.question_html: If the system of equations x + 4ay + az = 0, x + 3by + bz = 0 and x + 2 cy +cz = 0 have a non-zero solution,then a, b, c are in
questions.explanation: The system of linear equation has non trivial solution if : {tex}\begin{gathered} \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{4a}&a \\ 1&{3b}&b \\ 1&{2c}&c \end{array}} \right| = 0 \end{gathered}{/tex} {tex}\begin{gathered}\Rightarrow \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{4a}&a \\ 0&{3b - 4a}&{b - a} \\ 0&{2c - 4a}&{c - a} \end{array}} \right| = 0 \end{gathered} {/tex} {tex}\begin{gathered} \Rightarrow bc + ab - 2ac = 0 \end{gathered} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{2}{b} = \frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{c}{/tex} . Thus , a , b, c are in H.P.

questions.question_text: The matrix of the transformation 'reflection in the line x + y = 0 ‘ is
questions.question_html: The matrix of the transformation 'reflection in the line x + y = 0 ‘ is
questions.explanation: Suppose x' and y' be the reflection of x and y, therefore : {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {x'} \\ {y'} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1} \\ { - 1}&0 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - y} \\ { - x} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x'=-y,\;y'=-x{/tex} Therefore, reflection is on the line - x - y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y = 0.
